use_when: The Lab must monitor BTC regime hypotheses and falsify weak operational interpretations.
The value is public understanding, watch/reject/test status and boundary clarity, not trading advice.
do_not_use_when: The request asks for buy/sell calls, price targets, guaranteed returns, portfolio advice or regulated financial recommendations.
The available BTC data source cannot provide a data-card with source, retrieval timestamp, timeframe, window and missing-data handling.
The requested method cannot name the observable it measures and the null that can falsify it.

starter_observables.name: btc_log_return
starter_observables.role: Local price movement after scale normalization.
starter_observables.risk: Can turn selected windows into narrative if the split is chosen after the result.

starter_observables.name: realized_volatility
starter_observables.risk: Volatility is not direction and not edge.

starter_observables.name: drawdown_recovery_path
starter_observables.role: Shows stress depth and recovery structure.
starter_observables.risk: Can overfit crisis windows or exchange-specific anomalies.

starter_observables.name: range_trend_persistence
starter_observables.role: Distinguishes local trend/range hypotheses from baseline drift.

starter_observables.name: volume_or_liquidity_proxy
starter_observables.role: Warns about data quality and execution friction when available.
starter_observables.risk: Public proxies can be exchange-specific or incomplete.

starter_observables.name: volume_profile_poc
starter_observables.role: Declares the price level with maximum traded volume inside an explicit profile window.
starter_observables.risk: Manual or post-hoc window choice can manufacture support/resistance after the outcome.

starter_observables.name: low_volume_node_lvn
starter_observables.role: Marks a low-volume passage zone that may behave as an inefficiency corridor.
starter_observables.risk: Fill narratives can match arbitrary equal-width zones unless attacked by matched nulls.

starter_observables.name: fvg_or_imbalance_zone
starter_observables.role: Candidate zone where candles moved aggressively and left a mechanical gap/imbalance definition.
starter_observables.risk: Visual FVGs become subjective unless the candle rule, threshold and fill criterion are fixed before testing.

starter_observables.name: cme_gap_status
starter_observables.role: Tracks BTC futures weekend gaps with close/open timestamps and fill status.
starter_observables.risk: Historical fill rate can be overstated if time horizon and failed gaps are not included.

starter_observables.name: trendline_retest_event
starter_observables.role: Captures retest of a predeclared trendline with tolerance and invalidation rule.
starter_observables.risk: Trendlines are easy to redraw after the fact.

starter_baselines.name: random_walk_or_naive_drift
starter_baselines.role: Attacks naive predictability and direction stories.

starter_baselines.name: shuffled_returns
starter_baselines.role: Attacks claims that do not depend on temporal order.

starter_baselines.name: circular_block_shuffle
starter_baselines.role: Attacks results caused only by local autocorrelation.

starter_baselines.name: friction_baseline
starter_baselines.role: Attacks interpretations that disappear under spread, fee or latency assumptions.

starter_baselines.name: matched_random_level
starter_baselines.role: Attacks POC/retest claims by comparing against random levels at similar distance and volatility context.

starter_baselines.name: shuffled_volume_profile
starter_baselines.role: Preserves price path while breaking volume-profile structure to test whether POC/LVN adds information.

starter_baselines.name: equal_width_zone_fill_rate
starter_baselines.role: Attacks FVG/LVN fill claims by comparing against arbitrary zones of equal width.

starter_baselines.name: predeclared_forward_window
starter_baselines.role: Blocks after-the-fact inefficiency closure claims by fixing the test horizon before outcome.

starter_falsifiers.blocks: Using future information in feature, event or window selection.
Treating one visible BTC interval as a repeatable regime.
Calling a pattern meaningful when naive or shuffled baselines match it.
Importing an expert method before defining what it measures and how it fails.
Moving POC, trendline, FVG or LVN boundaries after price has moved.
Claiming gaps tend to close without counting all declared unfilled gaps and time horizons.
Mixing real traded volume, tick volume and exchange-specific proxy volume as if equivalent.
Turning watch/test zones into buy/sell signals before walk-forward and friction gates.

admin_boundaries.boundary: Run from current BTC seed and data-card; no direction override without review.
Expose source, window, nulls, falsifier flags and runtime dynamics.
This preset does not produce buy/sell/trading signals.

e2e_checks.expectation: A completed cycle updates regime hypothesis, baseline comparison and watch/reject/test status.
The UI exposes BTC source, timeframe, window, retrieval timestamp and missing-data handling.
No report or UI card uses buy/sell/price-target/trading-signal labels before a separate regulated product gate.
Any POC/LVN/HVN card exposes source, profile window, binning rule, tolerance and computed/manual status.
At least one FVG/LVN/CME-gap hypothesis has active/fill/invalidation status and a matched baseline comparison.

adaptation_questions: Which BTC data source is acceptable for the first public cycle?
Which timeframe and granularity should be monitored first?
Which human question should the Lab constrain rather than predict?
Which expert method, if any, is documented enough to be translated into observable, null and falsifier?
What should a human observer see in the dashboard after one useful cycle?
Which volume-profile source and binning rule are acceptable for a first POC/LVN cycle?
How is an inefficiency considered filled, partially filled or invalidated?
Which Alipio/Massimo Rea method should be translated first into observable, null and falsifier?
